claimText: Bharat Forecast System (BFS) is an indigenously developed high-resolution weather forecast system created by the Indian Institute of Tropical Meteorology (IITM), Pune.
evidenceExcerpt: Bharat Forecast System (BFS) is the world's first indigenously developed high-resolution weather forecast system, created by IITM, Pune.
examUse: Statement-based question on the developer of BFS.

claimText: The Bharat Forecast System (BFS) enhances India's weather prediction resolution to 6 km, positioning it ahead of models from the US, UK, and EU.
evidenceExcerpt: BFS improves India's weather prediction resolution from 12 km to 6 km, which is ahead of global models that operate at 9-14 km.
examUse: Statement-based question on the resolution capability of BFS compared to global models.
distractorHooks: Lags behind US and UK models

claimText: The Bharat Forecast System (BFS) integrates real-time data from 40 Doppler Weather Radars.
evidenceExcerpt: The system integrates real-time data from 40 Doppler Weather Radars, which use the Doppler effect to gather velocity data from particles.
examUse: Statement-based question on the data sources for BFS.

claimText: Arka and Arunika are High-Performance Computing (HPC) systems that power the Bharat Forecast System (BFS).
evidenceExcerpt: BFS is powered by High-Performance Computing (HPC) systems named Arka (IITM Pune) and Arunika (NCMRWF, Delhi).
examUse: Correct-pair question matching HPC systems to their locations or identifying them as supercomputers for weather forecasting.
distractorHooks: Arka and Arunika are new satellites
Arka is located at NCMRWF and Arunika at IITM

claimText: Mission Mausam was launched in 2024 by the Ministry of Earth Sciences (MoES) to improve weather and climate services.
evidenceExcerpt: Mission Mausam was launched in 2024 by the Ministry of Earth Sciences to improve weather and climate services.
examUse: Statement-based question on the nodal ministry for Mission Mausam.
distractorHooks: Launched by Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC)
Launched by Ministry of Science and Technology
